mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing sustained positive equity returns over a quarter-long horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within the following week during periods of high market volatility.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following INDPRO declines when VIX exceeds 20, or the signal shows zero predictive power in high-volatility subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicate tightening real interest rates that compress equity multiples, generating negative forward returns over the next month as discount factors increase.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS holds steady, with positive returns occurring in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year backtest, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening residential investment and reduced construction activity, triggering immediate negative market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 drawdowns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where HOUST drops below the 5% threshold, negating the predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy where supply constraints outpace labor absorption, leading to negative equity returns over 20 days as recession fears materialize.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% while UNRATE falls > 0.1pp simultaneously
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect disappears when tested against historical late-cycle periods.
